operating_state: Net revenues increased 7% to $12.70 billion in Q3 2024, driven by 20% higher investment banking revenues from debt and equity underwriting activity, 70% growth in net interest income from higher rates and balances, and 16% increase in Asset & Wealth Management revenues from net gains in equity investments and higher management fees. Market making revenues declined 19% due to lower interest rate products and commodities intermediation, while Platform Solutions revenues fell 32% from consumer platform wind-down activities.
cost_structure: Operating expenses decreased 8% to $8.32 billion in Q3 2024, primarily from the absence of prior-year GreenSky write-downs and lower commercial real estate impairments, partially offset by higher transaction-based expenses. Efficiency ratio improved to 65.5% from 76.6% in the prior year, while compensation rose due to improved performance while maintaining a 33.5% ratio to net revenues.
material_events: Goldman returned $1.98 billion to shareholders through share repurchases and dividends, bringing nine-month capital returns to $8.84 billion. The Board increased quarterly dividend from $2.75 to $3.00 per share beginning Q3 2024. The firm issued $5.5 billion in fixed/floating rate notes during July 2024 and launched a proposed Series Y preferred stock offering to redeem outstanding Series P preferred stock.
macro_exposures: Operations occurred amid inflation concerns, geopolitical tensions including Ukraine and Middle East conflicts, central bank policy uncertainty, and potential national election outcomes. The Federal Reserve implemented its first rate cut since 2022 while commercial real estate market concerns persisted. Investment banking activity levels generally declined while market-making activity increased with declining 10-year government bond yields and equity indices increasing 6%.
forward_outlook: Management targets through-the-cycle ROE of 14-16% and return on average tangible common shareholders' equity of 15-17% with 2024 effective tax rate of approximately 22%. The firm expects annual firmwide management fees exceeding $10 billion in 2024 and targets Platform Solutions pre-tax profitability by end of 2025. Asset & Wealth Management targets pre-tax margins in mid-twenties and ROE in mid-teens within three to five years.
uncertainty_profile: Provision for credit losses increased to $397 million from $7 million in the prior year, primarily from credit card portfolio charge-offs. Under 100% adverse economic scenario, allowance for credit losses would increase by approximately $0.8 billion. The Federal Reserve revised stress capital buffer to 6.2% effective October 2024, with potential G-SIB surcharge increase to 3.5% effective January 2026.
